solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    vector<int> lexicalOrder(int n) {
        vector<int> result;
        int num = 1;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            result.push_back(num);
            if (num * 10 <= n) {
                num *= 10;
            } else {
                while (num % 10 == 9 || num >= n) {
                    num /= 10;
                }
                ++num;
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution generates the lexicographical order by simulating the traversal of numbers as strings. It uses a single variable `num` to traverse the sequence, moving to the next lexicographical number by either multiplying by 10 or incrementing and backtracking when necessary. This approach ensures O(n) time and O(1) space.
